title: Staging and Grading Periodontitis
source: 2017 World Workshop on the Classification of Periodontal and Peri-Implant Diseases and Conditions
reference: Tonetti, Greenwell, Kornman. J Periodontol 2018;89 (Suppl 1): S159-S172

staging.description: Staging intends to classify the severity and extent of a patient's disease based on the measurable amount of destroyed and/or damaged tissue as a result of periodontitis and to assess the specific factors that may attribute to the complexity of long-term case management.
staging.notes: Initial stage should be determined using CAL. If CAL is not available, RBL should be used. Tooth loss due to periodontitis may modify stage definition. One or more complexity factors may shift the stage to a higher level.

severity.radiographic_bone_loss: Extending to middle third of root and beyond

additional_factors: Furcation involvement Class II or III

severity.radiographic_bone_loss: Extending to middle third of root and beyond

additional_factors: Need for complex rehabilitation due to:

grading.description: Grading aims to indicate the rate of periodontitis progression, responsiveness to standard therapy, and potential impact on systemic health.
grading.notes: Clinicians should initially assume Grade B disease and seek specific evidence to shift to Grade A or C. Whenever available, direct evidence should be used.

primary_criteria.case_phenotype: Heavy biofilm deposits with low levels of destruction
Destruction exceeds expectations given biofilm deposits; specific clinical patterns suggestive of periods of rapid progression and/or early onset disease
